questions.question_text: Is our reported MRR of $2.4M accurate or inflated by ghost accounts?
questions.answer_summary: A 20%+ MRR overstatement means we're reporting $480K in phantom revenue — this affects fundraising valuations and could constitute material misstatement to investors.

questions.question_text: Where are the ghost accounts coming from and how many are there?
questions.answer_summary: Ghost accounts inflate active user counts used in CAC and LTV calculations, distorting the unit economics that drive pricing and investment decisions.

questions.question_text: Which plan tiers have the most data quality issues?
questions.answer_summary: If data quality issues concentrate in Enterprise accounts, the revenue impact per affected account is 10x higher than in self-serve — prioritization must follow the money.

questions.question_text: What should engineering fix first — event deduplication or ghost account cleanup?
questions.answer_summary: Ghost account cleanup has a larger impact on reported metrics (MRR, active users, churn) and is prerequisite for accurate NRR calculation.

questions.question_text: Can we defend our churn numbers to the board?
questions.answer_summary: Churn rate discrepancies undermine board confidence in growth narratives — accurate churn is the foundation of every SaaS valuation model.
